title: workflow executor crashes with `name.replace is not a function` on any agent dispatch
body: Surfaced by the v0.24.x verification protocol run (docs/reports/pi-internal-verification-protocol-2026-05-02.md). Every agent-dispatching test section returns the runtime error `name.replace is not a function` from the workflow executor before the agent subprocess is spawned. Suspected location: packages/pi-workflows/src/workflow-executor.ts or packages/pi-workflows/src/dispatch.ts where a name-like field is normalised; investigation needed to identify the exact call site (likely a recent refactor introduced a value where a string was previously assumed). Blocks 13 of the protocol test sections (4.4, 4.5, 4.6, 4.7, 4.8, 4.10, 4.12a, 4.13c, 4.13e, 4.15, 4.16, 4.18, 5.1) and is the gating defect for REVIEW-001 dispatch via workflow (the intended shape uses an agent step then nestedAppend findings). Direct-tool surfaces (resolve-item-by-id, render-item-by-id, enforce-budget, append-block-nested-item, update-block-nested-item, remove-block-item, remove-block-nested-item, read-block-dir) all pass end-to-end so the defect is contained to the workflow executor agent-step path. One error message means one bug-shape; investigation should be bounded.
location: packages/pi-workflows/src/workflow-executor.ts (or sibling agent-dispatch site — exact line TBD by investigation)
